feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Filter items in a section based on a selected or checked item in another section on an Object Page
entry.content:
Greetings, community experts.

I'm working on a SAP CAP + SAP Fiori Elements application, which has an Object Page with several sections (all Tables).

I need to filter the table elements in the lower sections based on which item (row) is selected or highlighted in the table in the first section. The following image illustrates the idea a little more.

The goal is for a user to be able to click on a row in a table (in the example image, I clicked on the first row of the first table... that's why the line appears highlighted with a dotted box), and based on that, I can apply certain filters to the subsequent tables.

I don't know if what I'm suggesting is possible in SAP Fiori Elements, or where to start.

Any help, suggestions, related materials, or even proposed alternative approaches would be appreciated in advance.

entry.title: Error during request to remote service: Request failed with status code 404
entry.content:
How to resolve below error:

{
    "error": {
        "code": "502",
        "message": "Error during request to remote service: Request failed with status code 404"
    }
}

Background:
I have CAP NodeJS service which gets the data from remote/external service.
I am using XSUAA, OAuth2.0ClientCredentials authentication method for this service.
Also, mta.yaml is used for deployment on Cloud Foundry, where destination service and xsuaa service are configured.
It works as expected locally but but when I deployed on Cloud Foundry, it gives above error.

To configure hybrid/local testing, I have followed this documentation.
https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/using-services#connect-to-remote-services-locally

I am not sure the application has any conflict between service keys and bindings which I manually created to test real data on external service.
Can .cdsrc-private.json create any issue because I deployed into cloud foundry?
Is that fine when I have production profile and hybrid profile in CDS requires section in package.json file?

Tried to remove hybrid profile and rename of .cdsrc-private.json file and redeploy but still could not fix the issue.
Can anyone support on this?
Thanks.

package.json:

mta.yaml

Willem_Pardaens @vvdries @gregorw @qmacro

Reference:

Solved: Error during request to remote service: Failed to ... - SAP Community

Error during request to remote service: Failed to ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Calling a Remote Action from Event Handler in CAP Java
entry.content:
Hello together,

does anyone have an example or guidance on how to call a remote action from an event handler in CAP Java?

In Node.js, calling a remote action is quite straightforward, as shown in the official documentation:
👉 https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/core-services#consuming-services

However, in Java, I understand that it involves working with CQN and using the remote service proxy:
👉 https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/java/cqn-services/remote-services

Unfortunately, I haven’t found a concrete example yet. For instance, the SAP Cloud CAP Java samples don't seem to demonstrate calling a remote action specifically.

Has anyone implemented this or could share a snippet or an approach for calling a remote-bound action from within a CAP Java event handler?

Thanks a lot in advance and best regards,

Julian

entry.title: Is it common to use SAP BAS Productivity Tools in real business cases for CAP developement?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm currently following the SAP Learning Journey "Developing Full-Stack Applications Using Productivity Tools in SAP Business Application Studio". I find the tools quite intuitive and useful in a learning context, however, based on my experience across a few real-world CAP projects, I’ve rarely (if ever) seen these tools actually used in practice. Most teams I've worked with tend to rely on manual coding without touching the productivity features.

I'd really appreciate hearing about your experience or your team’s approach, especially if you've used these tools in production environments.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: CAP application with multiple databases (HANA + PostgreSQL)?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Is it possible to configure a SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) application to use more than one database?

For example:

  • For production, I want to have a primary database such as SAP HANA.

  • But I also want to be able to connect to a secondary database, like PostgreSQL, from within the same CAP application.

If this is possible, could you please provide an example of how the package.json configuration would look?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Logout functionality SAP CAP application deployed in Cloud Foundry using Managed approuter
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have built a SAP CAP application with UI5 and used managed approuter. The application is deployed in Cloud Foundry and I can access it through HTML5 applications. This application uses XSUAA authentication. 

I want to implement logout functionality in this application for the user. I have read many blogs and I'm unable to implement it. I have done things like making changes in xs-app.json file to include the logout parameter as described in the link:

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/logout

On the logout button in my app, i put the controller logic to 

window.location.replace("/my/logout").

After this when I click on logout, it takes me to a URL:
"https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/resources/sap-ui-logout-url" and when i click the back button, it again goes directly into the application logged in as the previous user, which means that it is not deleting the session maybe.

I even followed the question:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/logout-sap-ui5-app-cloud-foundry-sample-code/qaq-p/12248689

(Yes, I gave relative path in the controller script like my/logout as I am using managed approuter)

But when i do this, i get an internal server error after clicking on logout.

On checking the error logs of the app, I see the error as:
GET request to /74cf1c07-1827-49d2-9bc9-90ba4d9f6781.capleavemanagementappservice.leavemanagement-1.0.0/my/logout completed with status 500 request method (GET) does not match logout configuration logoutMethod (POST)


Does anybody know how can I implement a logout functionality in my application?

Thanks,
Raihan

entry.title: S4 Odata call from BTP CAPM application is failing
entry.content:
Hi experts

I have a custom odata call in s/4 system which is called from capm. its a post call configured to be called via destination and cloud connector. it was working fine for almost 2 years until recently where i keep getting teh following error in app log.

message: 'Error during request to remote service: Request failed with status code 403',
code: 'ERR_BAD_REQUEST',
response:
{
status: 403,
statusText: 'Forbidden',
headers: [Object [AxiosHeaders]],
body: 'CSRF token validation failed'
}

Nothing was changed either on the abap side on the odata or the capm side.

destination config is as follows,

"cds": {
        "requires": {
            "OdataApi": {
                "kind": "odata",
                "credentials": {
                    "destination": "DEST",
                    "path": "/sap/opu/odata/SAP/POST_PAY_SRV"
                },
                "csrf": true,
                "csrfInBatch": true
            },

Cloud connector, destination and user all are valid and calling the odata from postman is also working fine. 

Looks like csrf token fetch is not working as expected. (i do call the fetch token in a differnt call in postman and add it to the post call, which then works fine)

I am triggering the post call like below code

 

              const OdataApi = await cds.connect.to("OdataApi");

                const { pay } = OdataApi.entities;

                let payResp = await OdataApi.insert(PayItem).into(pay);

any pointers to solve this is very much appreciated,

thanks

entry.title: How to filter the list when the column is 1:n relation
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to create a webapp using SAP Business Application Studio in BTP. 

The app is connected to S4 HANA Cloud system using oData v2 API

I have view which is a list of products. I want to add a search box to filter the result by Production Description column. Production and Product Description has 1:n relation.

I am using the below source codes. But it doesn't work. 

I get the error in the console - Error during request to remote service: Left hand expression of memberaccess operator has wrong cardinality (to many not allowed)

Can someone please help me?

 

                var aTableSearchState =[];
                var sQuery = oEvent.getParameter("query");

                if (sQuery && sQuery.length > 0) {
                    var ProductTypeFilter = new Filter("ProductType", FilterOperator.Contains, sQuery);
                    var MaterialFilter = new Filter("Material", FilterOperator.Contains, sQuery);
                    var LanguageFilter = new Filter("to_Description/Language", FilterOperator.EQ, 'EN');
                    var DescriptionFilter = new Filter("to_Description/Description", FilterOperator.Contains, sQuery);
                    var QueryFilter1 = new Filter({ filters:[LanguageFilter, DescriptionFilter], and: true});
                    //var QueryFilter = new Filter({ filters:[ProductTypeFilter, MaterialFilter], and: false});
                    var QueryFilter = new Filter({ filters:[ProductTypeFilter, MaterialFilter, QueryFilter1], and: false});
                    aTableSearchState.push(QueryFilter);
                }
                this._applySearch(aTableSearchState);
 
         
_applySearch: function(aTableSearchState) {
          
           var oTable = this.byId("table"),
           oViewModel = this.getModel("worklistView");
            oTable.getBinding("items").filter(aTableSearchState, "Application");
            // changes the noDataText of the list in case there are no filter results
            if (aTableSearchState.length !== 0) {
                oViewModel.setProperty("/tableNoDataText", this.getResourceBundle().getText("worklistNoDataWithSearchText"));
            }

        }

entry.title: SAP CAP + Fiori Elements + fiori-tools-servestatic
entry.content:
Hello all,
 
we are facing some issues since a few days with loading a custom lib. It seems that a 
 
cds watch 
 
isn't serving paths of a Fiori elements app correctly which are configured over the middleware task fiori-tools-servestatic. The log says that it is served but over the browser we are not able to access it.
Over a ui serve of the app it's working.
 
server:
  customMiddleware:   
    - name: fiori-tools-servestatic
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        paths:
          - path: /testIT
            src: "/home/user/projects/myproject/test"
 
As a result custom libs can not be loaded in BAS / Build Code when running cds watch.
 
CAP version is 9, but it is not working with the latest 8 version neither. 
"cds-plugin-ui5": "^0.13.0" ist in use as well.
 
Anybody an idea what has changed?

entry.title: SAP CAP mock REST service
entry.content:
I have a CAP application which communicates with an external API  (for user information).

I have written some simple test cases. All working fine. Now the more complex stuff should be tested and I need to mock my API.

As best with a static json response I can rely on.

So I tried to realize that but can't get a working solution.

Found something that looked promising... 

https://github.com/SAP-samples/cap-service-integration-codejam/blob/main/exercises/06-mock-separate-process/README.md

But.. No luck with it.

entry.title: SAP CAP Navigating to an external navigation with Composition
entry.content:
So, I stumbled upon this tutorial to configure external navigation using link.

Configuring External Navigation

I noticed this works with association. What about composition? Does it work as well? In theory, the two are similar and should function normally. But I'm not sure. Has anyone ever done this?

Thanks!

entry.title: Connect SAP CAP app to another CAP app in a different space through a destination
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm currently trying to two CAP apps, which are hosted in different spaces, to each other.

I'll call the calling CAP app as AppA on SpaceA and the CAP app that provides the odata API which I'm trying to connect to as AppB on SpaceB.

To give a bit of background, AppB, internally connects to another API where having a user context is important. To achieve this I've decided to create a destination on AppA destination service, which has an authentication type of OAuth2JWTBearer, so the user context can be passed. To automatize the process of destination creation, I'm using mtaext to create the destinations automatically.

As part of the destination, I want to use credentials from AppA's XSUAA and not from AppB, since they are in different spaces and I cannot directly access AppB's XSUAA credentials ( unless I provide them manually). To achieve this, I tried to grant a scope of AppB to AppA so the AppA can query the odata API with their own credentials.

The configuration is the following:

AppB xs-security.json

{
  "xsappname": "AppBxsname",
  "tenant-mode": "dedicated",
  "scopes": [
    {
      "name": "$XSAPPNAME.ScopeToShare",
      "description": "Scope for AppA",
      "grant-as-authority-to-apps": [
        "$XSAPPNAME(application,AppAxsname)"
      ],
      "granted-apps": [
        "$XSAPPNAME(application,AppAxsname)"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "attributes": [],
  "role-templates": [],
  "oauth2-configuration": {
    "redirect-uris": [
      "http://*.localhost:5000/**",
      "https://*.hana.ondemand.com/**",
      "https://*.applicationstudio.cloud.sap/**"
    ]
  
}

AppA xs-security.json

{
    "xsappname": "AppAxsname",
    "tenant-mode": "dedicated",
    "scopes": [],
    "attributes": [],
    "role-templates": [],
    "authorities": [
        "$ACCEPT_GRANTED_AUTHORITIES"
    ],
    "foreign-scope-references": [
        "$ACCEPT_GRANTED_SCOPES"
    ]
}

AppA package.json

{
  ...
  "cds": {
    "features": {
      "kibana_formatter": true
    },
    "requires": {,
      "hana": {
        "deploy-format": "hdbtable"
      },
      "[production]": {
        "db": {
          "kind": "hana",
        },
        "auth": {
          "kind": "xsuaa"
        }
      },
      "AppBServ": {
        "kind": "odata",
        "model": "srv/external/AppBServ",
        "credentials": {
          "destination": "appb-destination",
          "path": "/v1/my-service"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

 

When executing the request to AppB I get the following error : The audiences of the token fit none of the supplied services. After debugging the token that is sent, I can see that the audiences does not contain the scope that should've been provided by AppB's xsuaa.

I assume, based on the documentation, that the property granted-apps is supposed to provide the scope to the apps I refer there. But the same seems to not be working properly and I cannot find why.

Any idea what may be missing?

 

Note: Before switching to a destination that has user context, I used a destination with OAuth2ClientCredentials and the same "worked", i.e, I was able to bypass the token validation since the Client token contained the scope provided by AppB as configured in grant-as-authority-to-apps.

entry.title: unable to see in HTML5 Application in SAP BTP COCKPIT Subaccount
entry.content:
cf html5-list showing 

But still unable to see in HTML5 Application in SAP BTP COCKPIT Subaccount.
Should I wait as it is in Trial Account ?

Hence Content Manager also not showing anything 


Please find the detail.

This is regarding my trial account 7bc94454trial.
 
Here the below UI5 app
"hanaodataservices.ui5fristapponhanaodata"
is not showing in the HTML5 Application under BTP Subaccount after successful deployment to cloud foundry, cf html5-list also showing the same & I have all the role collection access, Servcices deployed and entitlement and subscription intact.
 
Please find all the required screenshots.
 
region -us10

 

 
 

 

 
 

 


Need help on the same.

entry.title: node rfc server issue being unreliable
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I set up a node rfc server according to the official documentation. https://github.com/SAP-archive/node-rfc/blob/HEAD/doc/usage.md#nodejs-server-program-example

I set up the ini file, the rfc destination (which successfully passes the connection test), the sec_info, and reg_info.

I'm calling the Node.js RFC function from ABAP using a CALL FUNCTION ... DESTINATION statement.

The issue is sometimes it works and sometimes not. Once it executes the node js function I defined successfully then I restart the server again and then I get the error that the function module (name of the function) not found.

I could not find a reasonable pattern that under what condition it works and under what condition it does not.

I appreciate any help.

I can provide my full codes if needed.

Thanks in advance

@Srdjan

entry.title: CAP + Fiori Elements - Structure of app
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am pretty new to all the cloud development topics and have been assigned a project where I have to migrate a Z-Cockpit to the cloud as a Side-By-Side Extension. I have been trying around a little and would like to clear up some doubts I have in terms of viability or to double-check if might not be going in a completely wrong direction.

Status Quo:

I have build a FE app using the list report template and connected it to a CAP Backend (OData V4) which is consuming an OData-Service (V2) from a S4HC system. This OData-service is exposing some CBOs as well as some finance related data associated to the main entity. 

Requirement:

On the FE's object page there is a table displaying a sub-entity to the main entity. The user needs to enter data somewhere so that per trigger action (table action?) I can use the input data as well as the initial data of the sub entity to A create a finance document (SAP Standard) from my cap backend and B update the sub entity (CBO) on the S4HC system. The input data is necessary for the FI document creation.

My approach

1. I was considering declaring a new associated sub entity to the sub entity (so a sub-sub entity) to make it appear in the table as well and then editable - so that the user can enter data - on custom action button trigger my cap action and pass the values to the CAP action that I need.

2. On click on one of the rows of the table I navigate to a custom page where I create some custom entry fields - let the user enter data - and on custom button click trigger the CAP action and pass the values to the CAP action (Not sure if the data of the sub entity's row can be acessed here?)

I would appreciate it a lot if you could guide me here and give me some tips whether I am taking the right approach here or if this sounds rather like a SAPUI5 Freestyle case to you and if it indeed a viable case for FE which of 1 or 2 would be preferable or even suggest a better approach to me.

Big thanks in advance and regards,

Oliver

entry.title: VS Code CDS Language Support completion for i18n translation IDs is not fully implemented
entry.content:
Dear CDS VS code extension team,

I have tested to import an external library which I have added to the package.json as stated in the documentation but it appears that the VS code extension is not proposing the translation ID.

 

Here is part of my package json :

"cds": {
"i18n": {
"folders": [
"i18n",
"_i18n",
"/node_modules/my-i18ncommon-content/src/i18n"
]
 
At runtime the translation is properly made.
 
Thanks in advance for your support !
 
},

entry.title: Mock accesses to a DB Table having a vector field (CAP, node.js)
entry.content:
Hello,

I am using CAP in a node.js project (with  kyma & Hana Cloud, unit tests with Jest).

I have a DB table with embeddings as vectors, stored in HANA. 

To have a good code coverage, I'd like to be able to mock the calls to this table (sqLite cannot be used to have the table locally in an hybrid test profile unfortunately). I haven't found until now any better way to do this than encapsulate my DB accesses in static methods and mock these.

Is there any guidance from CAP on how this should be done? (I did not find one in Capire)

Thanks a lot!

Dewi

entry.title: CAP Application not mocking remote services on every second app start
entry.content:
Hello,

we are currently facing the issue accross multiple CAP projects, that during local development, mocking of remote services doesnt work as expected. 

Every second time our app restarts in local development during cds watch the remote mock services are not started.

We have observed that behavior during in multiple projects:
- It seems only to happen when trying to mock multiple remote services at the same time (3+)
- It seems to only occur in CAP node.js not Java
- It seems to be related to the cds.env not being populated properly.

We have first noted it down since cds 8.3.0

We can mitigate the problem by either causing the server to restart again or running the mocks on a different server seperatly, but a solution would be appreciated.

Best regards
Sören

entry.title: SAP CAP, HDI and Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello collegues,

For a customer we are developing a SAP CAP application with Hana capabilities. Before this project I have always used HDI container active on the same subaccount to connect CAP with Hana.

But now, we have two subaccounts:

  • Development: we develop SAPUI5 and CAP applications. HDI container service is not active.
  • Datasphere: we have the Datasphere service active and instance running.

We need to connect CAP application with Datasphere. I have asked to active HDI container service in Development subaccount but the manager rejected it.

So, my question is... is it possible to connect from CAP to Datasphere without HDI container in Development subaccount?

If the answer is yet, is it right to do and how can be achieved?

Thank you very much in advance Community!!!

BR,

Manuel
